response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong general software engineering experience across multiple domains, this role requires native-level Japanese language proficiency (a hard requirement absent from the resume), deep embedded firmware expertise, and specialized security technology knowledge. The resume shows primarily web/cloud infrastructure work in JavaScript/TypeScript with minimal C programming depth (6 months at National Instruments). No evidence exists of Linux kernel modification experience, embedded networking knowledge, security protocols (MACsec, 802.1X), hardware control, or any Japanese language capability. This represents a fundamental mismatch requiring years of retraining in both language and technical specialization.
strengths: 15+ years of proven software engineering across multiple platforms demonstrating adaptability and technical breadth
Linux system knowledge and DevOps infrastructure experience (GitLab, CI, Docker) showing foundational systems-level understanding
Some C/C++ exposure at National Instruments and demonstrated polyglot programming ability across JavaScript, DLang, Java, and other languages
concerns: No native-level Japanese language proficiency listed—a mandatory requirement; resume lists only English, Romanian, and German
Career trajectory focused on web/cloud systems (TypeScript, Node.js, high-level services) with only 6 months of C programming experience, insufficient for embedded firmware development
No evidence of embedded systems, firmware development, security technologies (encryption, authentication systems, MACsec, 802.1X), Linux kernel source modification, or network switch/device knowledge

key_factors.factor: C programming and embedded firmware expertise
Security technologies and embedded networking background
